required_claim_scores.required_claim: It relies on attention to draw dependencies between input and output.
It improves parallelization and achieves strong translation quality.
Experiments use WMT 2014 English-German and English-French data.
Sentences are tokenized with BPE or word-piece vocabularies and batched by approximate sequence length.
Training uses eight P100 GPUs and an Adam schedule with warmup.
No exact total energy-consumption value is reported in the paper.
BERT is a bidirectional Transformer-based language representation model.
Masked language modeling enables deep bidirectional pre-training.
BERT reduces reliance on task-specific architectures and improves results on eleven NLP tasks.
Every task is formulated as text input to text output.
The same model/objective/training/decoding process is reused across tasks.
The framework is called T5, the Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer.
The work is primarily a survey/exploration rather than a new-method proposal.
The coordinate-ascent study can miss second-order effects among design choices.
Some automatic benchmark improvements may not correspond to better qualitative behavior.
The first paper presents the Transformer as an encoder-decoder architecture for sequence transduction.
The second paper uses a bidirectional Transformer encoder as a representation model.
BERT adds pre-training and fine-tuning objectives rather than introducing the original Transformer architecture.
